title: campanula elatines var. subalpina
intro: Campanula elatines var. subalpina, also known as dwarf bellflower, is a perennial plant species that belongs to the Campanulaceae family, characterized by its small bell-shaped flowers in shades of blue and white. Traditionally, Campanula elatines var. subalpina has been used in herbal medicine, and its medicinal properties include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antiviral properties, which have been attributed to the presence of flavonoids and phenolic acids. The health benefits of Campanula elatines var. subalpina include its potential to aid in the treatment of respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its possible use in reducing inflammation and improving immune function. Campanula elatines var. subalpina can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and it is also used in some traditional remedies in the form of a poultice or salve.
